LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS FARMERS GROUP INC, FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FARMERS NEW WORLD LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE, MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TIME-BARRED. THE INSTANT ACTION STEMS FROM THE SAME ALLEGED BREACH OF CONTRACTS BETWEEN PLAINTIFF'S MEMBERS AND DEFENDANT THAT WAS ALLEGED IN THE PRIOR TEXAS ACTION FILED IN 1992. PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO BRING SUIT ON BEHALF OF ITS MEMBERS STEMS FROM THE RIGHTS OF ITS MEMBERS WHO ENTERED INTO THE CONTRACTS WITH DEFENDANT. THOSE CLAIMS LIE IN BREACH OF CONTRACT, PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO SUE ALSO LIES IN BREACH OF CONTRACT, AND THE 4 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLIES. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS THERE IS NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT MAY BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED. TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE WHY THIS ACTION IS NOT TIME-BARRED. (THE COURT ADDS HER REASONING AS FOLLOWS: DARG USED BY DEFENDANTS VIOLATES THE TERMS OF THE AGENCY APPOINTMENT AGREEMENT BROUGHT IN THE CASE PLAINTIFF FILED IN 1992. EVEN THOUGH THIS IS A DECLARATORY RELIEF ACTION, THIS CASE, LIKE THE 1992 CASE, ADDRESSES THE DARG & ASSERTS ITS UTILIZATION IS A BREACH OF THE AGREEMENT) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS FARMERS GROUP INC, FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FARMERS NEW WORLD LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE, MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: William Tedards, Jr., Attorney at Law, (202) 744-1214, appearing for Plaintiff United Farmers Agents Association, and opposing party; Royal F. Oakes, Esq. of Barger, Barger & Wolen LLP, (213) 680-2800, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TIME-BARRED. THE INSTANT ACTION STEMS FROM THE SAME ALLEGED BREACH OF CONTRACTS BETWEEN PLAINTIFF'S MEMBERS AND DEFENDANT THAT WAS ALLEGED IN THE PRIOR TEXAS ACTION FILED IN 1992. PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO BRING SUIT ON BEHALF OF ITS MEMBERS STEMS FROM THE RIGHTS OF ITS MEMBERS WHO ENTERED INTO THE CONTRACTS WITH DEFENDANT. THOSE CLAIMS LIE IN BREACH OF CONTRACT, PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO SUE ALSO LIES IN BREACH OF CONTRACT, AND THE 4 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLIES. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS THERE IS NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT MAY BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (Counsel to prepare Order)(302/CWW/PL)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2009 Continued To Oct-14-09 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FARMERS GROUP INC, FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FARMERS NEW WORLD LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE, and, MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Appearances: William P. Tedards, Esq., Attorney at Law, (202) 797-9135 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Royal F. Oakes, of Barger, Barger & Wolen LLP, (213) 680-2800, appearing for Defendants and moving party; Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Motion for Judgment on the pleadings is granted. Ten days leave to amend to allege why this action is not time-barred. (The Court adds her reasoning as follows: DARG used by Defendants violates the terms of the Agency Appointment Agreement brought in the case Plaintiff filed in 1992. Even though this is a declaratory relief action, this case, like the 1992 case, addresses the DARG & asserts its utilization is a breach of the agreement) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW/VC) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
